tasks.title: Secrets in .env.production (Stripe both modes, Chatwoot, mail)

prep.what: Create the production `.env.production` file on azteam-vps with all real secrets — Stripe live+test keys, Chatwoot token, SMTP credentials — before the deploy script runs.
prep.understand: Without this file the deploy will boot with null secrets or wrong Stripe mode. `CACHE_STORE=database` is required for `onOneServer/ShouldBeUnique` to work. Keep `APP_SCHEDULER_ENABLED=false` (the scheduler must stay OFF until reconciliation passes after deploy). File lives at `/opt/azteamai-system/.env.production`, chmod 600.
approach: SSH into azteam-vps (`ssh azteam-vps`).

Create/edit `/opt/azteamai-system/.env.production`: set `STRIPE_KEY`, `STRIPE_SECRET`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (both test+live modes), `CHATWOOT_API_TOKEN`, `MAIL_HOST/PORT/USERNAME/PASSWORD/FROM_ADDRESS`, `APP_URL=https://fin.azteam.cloud`, `CACHE_STORE=database`, `APP_SCHEDULER_ENABLED=false`.
Run `php artisan config:cache` after writing the file.
Spot-check: `php artisan tinker --execute='echo config("services.stripe.secret");'` — should not be null.
resources: Stripe dashboard → Developers → API keys (both live and test mode)
Chatwoot → Settings → API Access Token
Mail SMTP credentials from your email provider (check `.env.example` for field names)
SSH alias: azteam-vps

tasks.title: DO droplet deploy (app/queue/scheduler/TLS/MySQL8, scheduler OFF)
tasks.notes: Deploy TOPOLOGY = bare-metal LEMP on azteam-vps (NOT Docker — docker stack was reverted). Use `./deploy/redeploy.sh` (`vps-deploy.sh` is STALE). CACHE_STORE=database. Scheduler DISABLED until reconciliation passes.

prep.what: Deploy the AZ Team AI billing system to production at `https://fin.azteam.cloud` on azteam-vps using bare-metal LEMP (php8.4-fpm + supervisor + MySQL 8).
prep.understand: This is NOT a Docker deploy — the Docker stack was added and reverted (commit e6a1f23). The deploy script is `./deploy/redeploy.sh` (not the stale `vps-deploy.sh`). Stack: php8.4-fpm, supervisor for `queue:work` workers, cron for scheduler (off until t39 passes). The nginx + certbot pattern is the same as your `aztakeoff.azteamai.com` deploy. Scheduler must stay OFF — the `cutover_completed` flag (t53) must be set only after reconciliation.
approach: On azteam-vps: clone repo to `/opt/azteamai-system` via read-only deploy key (or `git pull` if already there). Run `./deploy/redeploy.sh`.
Create `azteamai` MySQL database if it doesn't exist: `mysql -u root -p -e 'CREATE DATABASE azteamai CHARACTER SET utf8mb4;'`. Migrations will run via the deploy script.
Set up nginx virtual host for `fin.azteam.cloud` pointing to `/opt/azteamai-system/public/`. Copy and adapt the `aztakeoff.azteamai.com` nginx config — same shape.
Run `certbot --nginx -d fin.azteam.cloud` for Let's Encrypt TLS.
Configure supervisor: add a `azteamai-worker.conf` program block for `php artisan queue:work --sleep=3 --tries=3`. Restart supervisor.
Smoke-test: `curl https://fin.azteam.cloud/health` → 200. Open the URL in a browser and verify the login page loads.
resources: `./deploy/redeploy.sh` in the repo root
nginx config for `aztakeoff.azteamai.com` on the VPS — copy and swap domain
Supervisor config files already on the VPS at `/etc/supervisor/conf.d/`
azteam-vps SSH alias
certbot already installed on the box (used for aztakeoff deploy)

tasks.title: Reconciliation gate: imported AR + recurring state matches azteam_crm ground truth
tasks.notes: `CrmImporter::reconcile()` (line ~277) already computes the comparison. Extract to public `ReconciliationGateService` + `billing:reconcile-cutover` artisan command. PASS is the only proceed signal to enable the scheduler.

prep.what: Build the `billing:reconcile-cutover` artisan command that verifies imported AR balances and recurring schedules match the azteam_crm source of truth. PASS = safe to go live.
prep.understand: The CRM import (t38) ran and data is now in the new system. `CrmImporter::reconcile()` at ~line 277 already has the comparison logic. You need to (1) extract it into a `ReconciliationGateService`, (2) wrap it in a rerunnable artisan command that prints a per-client breakdown table, and (3) exits 0 on PASS / 1 on FAIL. Two checks: Σ `balance_due` across all clients matches azteam_crm, AND next-charge dates per recurring project are correct.
approach: Open `app/CrmImporter.php` and read the `reconcile()` method carefully (line ~277).
Create `app/Services/ReconciliationGateService.php` with a `check(): ReconcileResult` method containing the reconcile logic.

Create `app/Console/Commands/ReconcileCutover.php` — calls the service, uses `$this->table()` to render per-client breakdown, exits 0 (PASS) or 1 (FAIL). Make it idempotent (safe to run multiple times).
Run `php artisan billing:reconcile-cutover` on the deployed system and read the output. Fix any discrepancies before enabling the scheduler.
resources: `app/CrmImporter.php` — find the `reconcile()` method (~line 277) for the existing logic
azteam_crm database connection (check `.env.production` for a second DB or connect directly)
Laravel artisan command docs: `$this->table()` for tabular output, `$this->error()` + `return 1` for fail
Existing import data from t38 is already in the deployed DB

tasks.title: Verify Stripe webhook CSRF exemption (e2e test)
tasks.notes: Exemption already coded in `bootstrap/app.php`. Need a test: POST `/webhooks/stripe` with signed payload + NO CSRF token → 200 not 419. Exact path match, no trailing slash.

prep.what: Write a Pest test that locks the Stripe webhook CSRF exemption — POST to `/webhooks/stripe` with a valid signature but no CSRF token must return 200, not 419.
prep.understand: The CSRF exemption is already coded in `bootstrap/app.php` (`validateCsrfTokens except ['webhooks/stripe']`). Without this test, someone could accidentally remove the exemption and break all live Stripe webhook delivery in silence. Exact path match matters — no trailing slash. The test uses Stripe's own signature generation so it's a realistic payload.
approach: Look at existing webhook tests in `tests/Feature/` for how Stripe payloads are built in this codebase.
Create `tests/Feature/Stripe/WebhookCsrfExemptionTest.php`.
Build a signed payload: use `\Stripe\WebhookSignature::generateHeader()` (or `\Stripe\Webhook::generateSignature()`) with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
POST to `/webhooks/stripe` (exact, no trailing slash) WITHOUT a CSRF token (no `withCsrf()` call). Assert the response is NOT 419 — accept 200 or any other non-CSRF error to be signature-agnostic.
Run `php artisan test tests/Feature/Stripe/WebhookCsrfExemptionTest.php` — should pass.
resources: `bootstrap/app.php` — the `validateCsrfTokens except [...]` block
Stripe PHP SDK: `\Stripe\WebhookSignature` or `\Stripe\Webhook` for test header generation
Existing `tests/Feature/` — find any test that hits a webhook route for the pattern
`STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` from `.env`

tasks.title: Durable job queue (lease/heartbeat/reclaim, window-aware, priority, cap)

prep.what: Build a Postgres-backed durable job queue with atomic claim (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`), heartbeat keep-alive, orphan reclaim, 5h window awareness, priority ordering, and concurrency cap — the engine the sweeper sits on.
prep.understand: Sweeper jobs run for minutes. If the worker dies, the job must be reclaimed and retried — only a DB-backed queue gives you that. `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` in Postgres is the race-safe way to claim exactly one job per worker. The 5h window means: don't start a job that would overrun the end of the activity window. Priority lets high-priority jobs jump the queue. Concurrency cap prevents all workers from stampeding on the same batch.
approach: Write the migration: `jobs` table with `id` (uuid), `type` (varchar), `payload` (jsonb), `status` (enum: queued/claimed/done/failed), `priority` (int, lower = higher priority), `leased_at` (timestamptz, nullable), `heartbeat_at` (timestamptz, nullable), `attempts` (int default 0), `created_at`.

Build `lib/queue/Queue.ts`: `enqueue(type, payload, priority?)`, `claim(workerId, timeoutMs)` using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, `heartbeat(id)`, `release(id, success|failure)`, `reclaim()` (finds `status=claimed AND leased_at < now() - timeoutMs` → resets to queued).
Add `canStart(estimatedDurationMs): boolean` — checks `now() + estimatedDurationMs < windowEnd`.

Write tests: two concurrent workers claiming the same queued job → only one wins; orphan job reclaimed after heartbeat timeout; priority ordering; window gate rejects when time remaining < estimatedDuration.
Run `bun test` — all green.
resources: Postgres `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` — the canonical pattern for pg-backed queues (search 'postgres job queue FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED')
`lib/pg-board-repository.ts` in this project — how the Postgres client is used here
Bun test runner: `bun test` or `bun test lib/queue/`
`VPS_POSTGRES_URL` in `.env` for the `todos_platform` DB connection

tasks.title: Writer docker integration test on a real onboarded repo
tasks.notes: `refreshRepo` orchestration is unit-proven with mocked runners. Add ONE real in-image prep run against an onboarded repo to prove the docker transport end-to-end. The injected `RefreshRunners` seam makes this a ~30-min job once a repo is onboarded.

prep.what: Add one Docker integration test that runs `refreshRepo()` against a real onboarded repo using `DockerRefreshRunners` — not mocks — and verifies the prep outputs actually exist.
prep.understand: All existing sweeper tests mock the `RefreshRunners`. This test plugs in the real Docker runner to prove the transport end-to-end: repo is pulled, `prepareCommands` run inside the container, `prepareOutputs` exist after. The `RefreshRunners` injection seam (already in the code) makes this trivial. Pick Module-project — it already has a proven `prepareCommands/prepareOutputs` recipe in `SWEEPER_REPOS`.
approach: Open `SWEEPER_REPOS` config and find the Module-project entry — note its `prepareCommands` and `prepareOutputs`.
Create `tests/integration/writer-docker.test.ts`. Import `refreshRepo` and `DockerRefreshRunners`.
Call `await refreshRepo(moduleProjRepoMapping, new DockerRefreshRunners())` in the test.
Assert: each path in `prepareOutputs` exists on disk, the freshness marker file is written, no exception thrown.
Run: `bun test tests/integration/writer-docker.test.ts` — green means docker transport proven.
resources: `lib/sweeper/refresh.ts` — the `refreshRepo` function and its `RefreshRunners` interface
`SWEEPER_REPOS` config — Module-project entry with `prepareCommands` and `prepareOutputs`
`DockerRefreshRunners` implementation (find it in `lib/sweeper/`)
Docker must be running locally or on the VPS where you're running tests
